text: The surgeon begins the procedure by lysing adhesions between the previous sleeve gastrectomy and the liver. The dilated proximal part of the sleeve is visible. A posterior window is created behind the sleeve, followed by transection proximal to the stricture to form a gastric pouch. The stricture is clearly visible, and a stapler is used to transect the sleeve distal to the stricture, resulting in its resection at the pylorus level.

The surgeon retracts the transverse colon to identify the Treitz's angle. Approximately thirty centimeters from this angle, the jejunum loop is transected. The alimentary limb is measured to approximately one hundred twenty centimeters, where a side-to-side jejuno-jejunostomy is performed using a stapler.

The surgeon advances the alimentary limb cranially and performs a gastrojejunostomy. A running posterior suture of 3-0 PDS is used to start the anastomosis. After completing the outer-posterior layer, the gastric pouch is opened, and an enterotomy is created using a monopolar hook. An inner-posterior layer of 3-0 PDS is then sutured.

The surgeon begins suturing the anterior aspect of the anastomosis using a running 3-0 PDS suture. A second running suture is initiated from the opposite side, joining the previous suture in the middle, and all are tied together. After completing the inner and anterior layer, a second layer is added on top using a running suture.
